title: Catedrele titularilor nu au fost afectate. Sigur?
summary: A Romanian liberal commentator examines how increasing teaching hours by 2 hours has negatively impacted tenured teachers, particularly those without political party affiliations, who lose class hours to politically connected colleagues. The article critiques the lack of proper mentoring systems in Romanian education and calls for continuous teacher coaching as a solution.
talking_points: Increasing the teaching norm by 2 hours has led to tenured teachers losing parts of their teaching load, with politically unaffiliated teachers being disadvantaged
Optional courses created to fill gaps are designed around teacher profiles rather than student needs, further overloading student schedules
Political affiliation of school directors and inspectors influences which teachers retain full positions
Romania's teacher mentoring rates are far below European averages, with only 24% of beginning teachers receiving a mentor according to TALIS 2024
Continuous mentoring and transitioning from teacher training to teacher coaching would improve retention and student outcomes
author: Corina Atanasiu
